item.title: Lawmakers press Trump for military support for Taiwan after Xi summit
item.description: President Trump told reporters he made "no commitment either way" to China's Xi Jinping regarding U.S. weapons sales to Taiwan.

item.title: Trump talks up trade deals with China, but experts see no big wins for U.S.
item.description: President Trump's trip to China could bolster economic relations, but failed to deliver a breakthrough deal, some trade and energy experts said.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo kills dozens, Africa's CDC says
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak in a remote province in Congo has caused 65 deaths and 246 suspected cases so far, Africa CDC says.

item.title: 1 firefighter dead, 10 other people injured in explosion at Maine lumber mill
item.description: Firefighters responded to a fire at Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, about 95 miles from Portland.

item.title: Prominent N.Y. synagogue targeted in alleged terror plot, prosecutors say
item.description: An Iraqi national allegedly plotted to carry out terror attacks in the U.S., including at a prominent synagogue in New York, prosecutors said Friday.

item.title: Driverless Waymo cars get into traffic jam in Atlanta
item.description: Atlanta residents woke up to Waymo traffic jams on Friday. The driverless cars took over a quiet cul-de-sac. Skyler Henry has more details.

item.title: What did Trump, Xi accomplish from China summit?
item.description: President Trump has wrapped his high-stakes summit with China, calling it "very good," but it's hard to tell exactly what the takeaway is. Ed O'Keefe has more details.

item.title: SpaceX launches cargo ship to International Space Station
item.description: The International Space Station-bound SpaceX Cargo Dragon is loaded with 6,500 pounds of needed equipment, research gear and crew supplies.

item.title: Health officials battle new Ebola outbreak: Doctor who survived virus speaks out
item.description: Doctors are monitoring an Ebola epidemic in Africa that may be to blame for dozens of deaths so far. Lilia Luciano reports and spoke with a U.S. doctor who recovered from the virus.

item.title: Wind-driven wildfires rip across Texas Panhandle
item.description: Powerful winds fueled wildfires that raced through the Texas Panhandle this week. Firefighters are working around the clock to get the upper hand. Jason Allen reports and Rob Marciano has the forecast.

item.title: Trump returns to White House from China after 2-day summit
item.description: President Trump returned to the White House on Friday night after two days of high-level talks in China. CBS News foreign correspondent Anna Coren has the latest.

item.title: Explosion rips through historic Maine lumber mill, injuring at least 11
item.description: A massive explosion at a historic lumber mill in Maine injured multiple people on Friday. Ash-har Quraishi reports.

item.title: Terror plot targeting Jewish institutions foiled, suspect under arrest
item.description: A terror plot targeting Jewish institutions in New York, California and Arizona has been foiled, and a man linked to the alleged scheme is under arrest on Friday. Prosecutors say the Iraqi suspect is tied to other global attacks and claim he wanted payback for the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Iran says it "cannot trust" U.S. in negotiations as Trump says "we control" strait
item.description: Speaking to reporters on Air Force One, President Trump also claimed to have "wiped out" Iran's armed forces.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Virginia Democrats' bid to revive new congressional map
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday rejected a bid by Virginia Democrats to revive its new voter-approved congressional map that was drawn to advantage the party for the upcoming midterm elections.

item.title: Colorado governor commutes Trump ally Tina Peters' prison sentence
item.description: Colorado's governor on Friday announced he is commuting the sentence of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters.

item.title: Major Garrett gifted championship ring from Padres fantasy camp
item.description: In January, CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett went to Padres fantasy camp, where he says he had the time of his life. Every member of his team got a championship ring courtesy of the San Diego Padres. Managing editor of the CBS News Washington, D.C., bureau, Arden Farhi, tagged along for the outing and joins "The Takeout" to recount the experience.

item.title: How health officials are responding to Congo Ebola outbreak
item.description: Africa's top public health body has confirmed a new and deadly Ebola outbreak in a remote Congo province. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: Recapping Trump's China summit with Xi Jinping as Taiwan control takes center stage
item.description: President Trump has wrapped up his trip to Beijing, where he met face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Natalie Brand report.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: 2003 Space Shuttle Columbia disaster
item.description: On Feb. 1, 2003, the Space Shuttle Columbia disintegrated upon reentering Earth's atmosphere, killing all seven crew members on board. Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio's coverage from that day.

item.title: Iraqi man charged with planning U.S. terror attack on synagogue, prosecutors say
item.description: An Iraqi national was arrested and charged with planning to carry out several terror attacks in the U.S., including at a New York City synagogue and other prominent Jewish centers in Los Angeles and Scottsdale, Arizona. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Michigan Democratic Senate candidate Mallory McMorrow calls GOP rival a "formidable competitor"
item.description: The Democratic primary for the Michigan Senate race is heating up with less than three months to go before the election. Candidate and state Sen. Mallory McMorrow joins "The Takeout" to make her case for the upper chamber seat.

item.title: Betting on War | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: More than $1 billion has been wagered on prediction markets this year on military decisions and outcomes. This Sunday, 60 Minutes investigates suspiciously timed bets with information that appears too specific for civilian outsiders.

item.title: Boeing says China committed to buy at least 200 aircraft after Trump-Xi summit
item.description: President Trump is heading back to Washington, D.C., after his Beijing summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The president touted "fantastic trade deals" made between the U.S. and China. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has the details.

item.title: Breaking down Supreme Court decision preserving mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday issued a decision that will allow the abortion pill mifepristone to continue being sent through the mail for now. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks down the ruling.

item.title: Africa CDC confirms new Ebola outbreak in Congo
item.description: The Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has confirmed a new deadly Ebola outbreak in Congo that has claimed the lives of at least 65 people. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins with analysis.

item.title: Rep. Ro Khanna says he's "concerned that Trump may be compromising" U.S. assurances to Taiwan
item.description: President Trump wrapped up a two-day summit in China with future arms sales to Taiwan in some doubt and scant details on future Chinese purchases of American jets and soybeans. Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna of California joins "The Takeout" with his reaction to the trip.

item.title: 5/15: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Iraqi national charged with planning terror attacks; President Trump touts "fantastic trade deals" as Xi summit ends.

item.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes rare trip to Cuba as island faces energy crisis
item.description: The meeting came as Cuba is contending with a massive power failure to its national energy grid amid U.S. sanctions that have caused an oil and gas shortage crisis.

item.title: Extended interview: Viola Davis
item.description: In this web exclusive, Oscar-winning actress Viola Davis talks with Tracy Smith about co-authoring her first novel in collaboration with James Patterson, "Judge Stone." In her discussion of writing, acting and art, she notes that discomfort and awkwardness are "how we make the greatest discoveries in life."
